mappings.rationale: Directly covers the structures of the human body including skeletal, muscular, and organ systems outlined in this section.
Directly covers the functions of all major body systems including cardiovascular, respiratory, digestive, endocrine, nervous, and excretory systems.
Covers the central nervous system, brain structure and function, directly relevant to section J on the nervous system.
Directly covers blood components (plasma, erythrocytes, leukocytes, thrombocytes), blood groups, clotting, and circulation.
Covers the lymphatic system including lymphocytes, lymph nodes, and immune function, directly relevant to section B.
Directly covers the endocrine system including thyroid, adrenal, pituitary, and pancreatic islet secretions, and hormonal effects on reproduction.
Directly covers the eye and the process of vision, corresponding to section J.4.
Covers the fundamental unit of body tissues, relevant to understanding the composition and properties of body tissues and all organ systems.
Enzymes are essential to digestive processes, metabolic functions, and the biochemical operations of every body system covered in this section.
Relates to the digestive system's functions and the metabolic processing of nutrients through the gastrointestinal tract.
Provides understanding of the molecular basis of body fluids, tissues, and the biochemical processes underlying all body system functions.
Covers the biochemical processes underlying body system functions, including metabolism, enzyme action, and the composition of body fluids and tissues.
Directly covers the olfactory sense, one of the sensory receptors discussed in section J.6.e.
Covers dental structures, part of the mouth and related structures in the digestive system (section D.1.a) and the integument derivatives (section H.8).
Relevant to cutaneous senses (section J.6.a) and the nervous system's role in pain reception and processing.
Provides the molecular basis for understanding gene expression in different tissues and the molecular processes underlying all body system functions.
Covers the biological rhythms regulated by the endocrine and nervous systems, including the pineal gland and hypothalamus.
Explains how body structures develop and differentiate into the various organ systems and tissues described in this section.
Addresses the human body as a whole system, relevant to the integrative understanding of body structures and functions.
Relevant to understanding tissue regeneration, cell differentiation, and the formation of different body tissues and organ systems.
The human microbiome is integral to digestive system function, immune responses, and the composition of body tissues.
